records.ety: [AS. cofa room; akin to G. koben pigsty, orig., hut, Icel. kofi hut, and perh. to E. cobalt.]

defs: A retired nook; especially, a small, sheltered inlet, creek, or bay; a recess in the shore.
quotes: Vessels which were in readiness for him within secret coves and nooks.

defs: A strip of prairie extending into woodland; also, a recess in the side of a mountain.
A member, whose section is a concave curve, used especially with regard to an inner roof or ceiling, as around a skylight.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Coved (k?vd); p. pr. & vb. n. Coving.]

defs: To arch over; to build in a hollow concave form; to make in the form of a cove.
quotes: The mosques and other buildings of the Arabians are rounded into domes and coved roofs.

records.ety: [CF. F. couver, It. covare. See Covey.]

defs: To brood, cover, over, or sit over, as birds their eggs.
quotes: Not being able to cove or sit upon them [eggs], she [the female tortoise] bestoweth them in the gravel.

records.ety: [A gypsy word, covo that man, covi that woman.]

defs: A boy or man of any age or station.
quotes: Now, look to it, coves, that all the beef and drink Be not filched from us.
